notes: Doc 1593 is a minimalist man going to a gala/exhibition. Doc 1337 is an androgynous person with gender-neutral minimalist style. Doc 2623 is a cis woman preferring gender-neutral styles. Other results were minimalist but not gender-neutral.

notes: All results describe pear-shaped women. Strong matches across the board.

notes: All results are camping/hiking occasion entries. All relevant regardless of body type, since the query focuses on activity and weather.

notes: Doc 1852 seeks an hourglass silhouette. Doc 836 is a curvy woman with hourglass shape. Doc 2412 is a curvy woman with defined waist. Docs 584 and 1572 describe ruler-straight figures wanting to create curves, which is the opposite of the query.

notes: Only doc 732 (petite slender frame) is genuinely relevant. Doc 1109 describes a 5'11 woman (not petite). Other results are not petite or not festival-related. Weak coverage for this query.

notes: All results are women's black dresses. Doc 12423 is specifically labeled as formal. High-quality matches.

notes: All results are men's blue shirts for summer casual use. Score 0.89 indicates near-perfect match.

query: outfit for wheelchair user at a formal event
notes: ADVERSARIAL: No documents in the corpus address wheelchair accessibility. All returned results are irrelevant despite scores around 0.44. This demonstrates the system's inability to indicate when it lacks relevant data.

ADVERSARIAL: Neither dataset contains price information. The misleadingly high score (0.61) is a false positive. The system matched general outfit recommendations while ignoring the budget constraint entirely.
